title: acaena decumbens
intro: Acaena decumbens, also known as creeping katipo or woolly button, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenoid compounds. The health benefits of Acaena decumbens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Acaena decumbens can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
